title: Replace estimated total laps with causal FastF1 lap-count state
summary: The best current code infers total race length from LapNumber/RaceProgress.
rationale: The best current code infers total race length from LapNumber/RaceProgress. FastF1 exposes the intended TotalLaps during the session and notes that it can change, so a causal join should give cleaner finish-window and mandatory-stop features than a rounded back-calculation.
implementation_hint: Build a Year/Race/CurrentLap map from FastF1 lap_count, using the total-lap value known at that lap, not the final post-race total. Recompute LapsRemaining, can-finish margins by compound, and stop-debt features from this exact state.
expected_effect: Should reduce noise in all finishability features and help most in shortened races, testing sessions, and late-race one-stop/two-stop boundary cases.
risk: Using final TotalLaps instead of the value known at the current lap would leak future race-control outcomes. Race-name alignment may also be messy.
